message.content:
<deliberation>
I reviewed each main REG node (REG-008, REG-010, REG-040) against the provided policy chunk. There were no cross-references supplied, so considered_references are empty for all items. I looked for explicit contradictions or narrowings, not mere omissions (treated as Non-Deviation per the instructions). I found one actionable deviation under REG-008 where the regulation’s requirement that testing shall ensure compliance contrasts with the policy’s allowance to proceed with a gap-management plan when criteria are unmet. For REG-010 and REG-040, the policy either aligns in part or is silent; I did not identify explicit contradictions.
</deliberation>
```json
[
  {
    "reg_id": "REG-008",
    "clause": "Art9(6)",
    "considered_references": [],
    "deviation_found": true,
    "deviations": [
      {
        "deviation_type": "Execution style deviation",
        "gdpr_quote": "Testing shall ensure that high-risk AI systems perform consistently for their intended purpose and that they are in compliance with the requirements set out in this Section.",
        "policy_quote": "If there are Responsible Release Criteria for metrics or rubrics that have not been met, consult with the reviewers named in the Impact Assessment, and in the case of Sensitive Uses, with the Office of Responsible AI, to develop a plan detailing how the gap will be managed until it can be closed. Document that plan.",
        "mismatch": "The regulation requires testing to ensure compliance, whereas the policy contemplates managing unmet criteria via a gap-management plan rather than ensuring compliance through testing.",
        "analysis": "By allowing a managed plan for unmet criteria, the policy’s execution approach does not guarantee that testing ensures compliance as the Article requires."
      }
    ]
  },
  {
    "reg_id": "REG-010",
    "clause": "Art9(8)",
    "considered_references": [],
    "deviation_found": false,
    "deviations": []
  },
  {
    "reg_id": "REG-040",
    "clause": "Art15(3)",
    "considered_references": [],
    "deviation_found": false,
    "deviations": []
  }
]
```
